calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Mar-19-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Hyunson Ahn for Soon Yi Cho.

Mr. Ahn asked the court to permit a lien, instead of allowing the client more time to come up with the money, stating that she has been uncooperative. The court provided the amount of fees that is to go to the former attorney and the amount that is to go to Mr. Ahn. The Petition for Final Distribution, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Feb-26-2024 Continued To Mar-18-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jan-22-2024 Continued To Feb-26-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Hyunson Ahn appeared by zoom for Soon Yi Cho (personal representative).

Mr. Ahn stated that he has sent three letters asking the personal representative to pay his fees, that he has not received any response, and proposed placing a lien on the real property for the amount owed to the probate referee of $1,700.00 plus his attorney's fees totaling less than $16,000.00 all together with a 5% annual interest rate. Mr. Ahn also stated that he sent a letter to the previous attorney, but he has not received anything, and that the personal representative also ignored the previous attorney. The court noted that under the probate code the probate referee does not have to do a lien, that the estate cannot close and asked Mr. Ahn if he is willing to advance the $1,700.00 to the probate referee and roll it all over into his lien. Mr. Ahn responded "yes". The court stated that it needs to hear from Mr. Weinberg and that if Mr. Weinberg agrees, and Mr. Ahn provides a proposed order along with proof of payment to the probate referee, there may be no need for an amendment. The Petition for Final Distribution, etc. is continued to February 26, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Nov-13-2023 Continued To Jan-22-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Hyunson Ken Ahn appeared via zoom for Soon Cho (petitioner).

Discussion was held regarding the statutory fees, that attonry is willing to discount $30,000.00 fees, and that to make sure the referee also gets paid. Petition for final distribution continued to January 03, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross Moody: Clerk: A. Datt: Deputy: Barrett: Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Sep-25-2023 Continued To Nov-13-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom:
Hyunson Kenneth Ahn, Esq. for Soon Yi Cho, Adminstrator.

The Court addressed Mr. Ahn stating that he was sent notes by the probate examiner. Mr. Ahn stated that he explained to his client that the appraisal fee needs to be paid, that the balance needs to be shown, and that both have not been done yet. Mr. Ahn also stated that he had e-filed the proposed order and requested a continuance of six or seven weeks. The Court inquired if Mr. Ahn received the list of issues sent to him by the probate examiner. Mr. Ahn confirmed receipt of the examiner's notes. The Court briefly addressed the issues raised in the notes including the need to correct the proposed order and attach the legal description of the property, the need to pay the probate referee's commission of $1,771.00 and submit proof, and an allegation made that sufficient cash has been advanced to the estate for payment of attorney's fees and costs. Mr. Ahn stated that he would talk to his client.

The matter is continued to November 13, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 for counsel to address the issues raised in the examiner's notes.

Judge: Cecilia Castellanos. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Singh. Matter not reported.

Off Calendar Dec-20-2022 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Nov-30-2022 Continued To Dec-20-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Nov-02-2022 Continued To Nov-30-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Hyunson K. Ahn, via Zoom, for Soon Y. Cho, Personal Representative.

Mr. Ahn reports of delays in this matter caused by the pending response to letters he sent to parties in Korea regarding acknowledging that they will not receive any assets in distribution. The court advises that it doesn't believe that the preceding is required and confirms that the decedent was survived by the spouse, the Personal Representative, and a child. The court reiterates that it doesn't believe that the acknowledgment of the parties in Korea is required but does not discourage counsel to proceed as he wishes but reminds counsel that it should not delay the filing of a petition.

After additional discussion, the matter is continued to Nov-30-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. Counsel is advised that his appearance will not be required if a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is filed by Nov-23-2022.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Cristobal. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Oct-11-2022 Continued To Nov-02-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Oct-05-2022 Continued To Oct-11-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Jul-27-2022 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jul-27-2022 Continued To Oct-05-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Hyunson K. Anh, in person, for Soon Y. Cho, Personal Representative.

Counsel offers testimony regarding his failure to appear at the last calling of this matter and advises that his client could not appear today. Counsel is reminded that the court ordered that both he and his client appear today. The court reminds counsel that it needs either a petition for final distribution or petition to approve the status of administration. Counsel confirms the preceding and advises of a handful of beneficiaries located in Korea.

After additional conversation, the Status on Close of Estate is continued to Oct-05-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. The Order to Appear is taken off calendar and the Order to Show Cause is discharged by the court.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jun-29-2022 Continued To Jul-27-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear and Order to Show Cause will be issued to the Administrator Soon Yi Cho and the Attorney Hyunson Kenneth Ahn to appear on July 27, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to show cause as to why sanctions in the amount of $250.00 should not be imposed against each individual the Administrator and the Attorney for failure to appear and for failure to file either a Petition for Final Distribution, or a Petition for Approval of Report on Status of Administration. Status on close of estate is also continued to that date.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: H. Singh. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar May-31-2022 Continued To Jun-29-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Feb-01-2022 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Dec-28-2021 Continued To Feb-01-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jonathan Weinberg appeared by zoom for Soon Cho.

Mr. Weinberg stated that he spoke with Ms. Cho last week, that she received the motion, that the person that was assisting Ms. Cho with translation no longer wants to do so, that Ms. Cho has engaged new counsel and that he signed a substitution of attorney, but it is not filed. Mr. Weinberg also stated that he filed the motion because at this point, they have a language barrier and that to the best of his knowledge, the property has not been encumbered or transferred. The Motion for Withdrawal of Attorney of Record is continued to February 1, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
